claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

**Let's start with the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm actually asking about two children - my eldest Alex is 18, and my younger one Blake is 13.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
It's great to meet you! Thank you for providing those details. I'll assess eligibility for both Alex (18) and Blake (13) for you.

Let's start by going through the questions for each child. Since they're in different age categories, the requirements will be slightly different.

---

**Starting with Blake (13 years old):**

Since Blake is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement. Let me check the next requirement for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (age 13):**

Is Blake working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?

(This is typically the case for 13-year-olds, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't work at all - they're only 13, still at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Blake is still at school and not working, so that's a yes - Blake is working less than 24 hours per week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (age 13):**

Is Blake **not** receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

(Again, this would be unusual for a 13-year-old, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Blake doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (age 13):**

Is Blake currently in an apprenticeship?

(I'm guessing not, since they're 13 and at school, but just to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in an apprenticeship - they're just in secondary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I expected. Blake is not in an apprenticeship, so we can continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status:**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? (You only need to meet **one** of these):

1. **Normally live in the UK**
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Have pre-settled status** and actively seeking work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. **Have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **Have pre-settled status** and studying with sufficient resources
7. **Have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **Crown servant** living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. You mentioned you're living abroad. Let me ask a follow-up question:

Are you living abroad as a **Crown servant** (for example, a UK diplomat, member of the armed forces serving overseas, or a civil servant posted abroad)? Or are you living in certain qualifying countries due to Crown service?

If not, could you tell me a bit more about your situation - for example, do you have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just live abroad with my family - I don't have any special status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being clear about your situation. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you don't meet any of the UK residency requirements needed to claim Child Benefit.

Since you:
- Don't normally live in the UK
- Don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Aren't a Crown servant living abroad

This means you wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit for either Blake or Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on your situation, I've reached an outcome for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Outcome

### Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either of your children at this time.

---

### Summary by Child:

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 18 | ❌ No | Claimant does not meet UK residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 13 | ❌ No | Claimant does not meet UK residency requirements |

---

### Why?

To claim Child Benefit, you must meet UK residency requirements. This means you need to:

- **Normally live in the UK**, OR
- Have **settled or pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), OR
- Be a **Crown servant** living abroad

Since you live abroad and don't meet any of these special circumstances, you unfortunately cannot claim Child Benefit.

---

### What You Can Do:

- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible at that point
- **If you believe your situation qualifies** under one of the special categories, you can contact HMRC directly to discuss your case
- For more information, visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry this wasn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex (age 18): Although Alex is within the eligible age range (16-19), the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not meet any other qualifying residency criteria. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 13): Blake meets the child-related criteria - they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not meet any other qualifying residency criteria. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, a claimant must normally live in the UK, or meet special circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or be a Crown servant living abroad. The claimant confirmed they live abroad, are not a Crown servant, and have no special immigration status that would qualify them.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
